title: VMware NSX Distributed Firewall Rule Hit-Rate Baselining, Counter Spike/Drop Detection, Stale-Rule Scoring, and Effectiveness Hints
exclusions: Does **not** replace **UC-18.2.1** east-west ALLOW/DROP **volume** economics and logging-mode mix trending—that sibling stays the flow-telemetry wide lens. Does **not** implement **UC-18.2.7** desired-vs-realized policy revision governance—no revision_id baseline diffs here. Does **not** cover **UC-18.2.2** NSGroup membership vs verdict enforcement gaps. Does **not** replace Gateway Firewall north-south policy optimization (Edge VM form factors live outside kernel DFW hit semantics). Does **not** score NSX Intelligence ML recommendations themselves—only counter realities they should eventually match.
evidence: Saved **`uc_18_2_6_nsx_dfw_rule_hit_rate`** scheduled search, Summary **`summary_nsx_dfw_hits`**, and Dashboard Studio **`ds_nsx_dfw_rule_effectiveness`** archiving weekly PNG bundles with **`domain_nm`** / **`rule_ref`** metadata.
dataSources: `index=vmware` or `index=vmware_nsx` or `index=network_sec` — **five** sourcetypes: **`vmware:nsxt:rule_stats`** (Policy **`GET .../rules/{rule}/statistics`** plus legacy **`/api/v1/firewall/sections/{section}/rules/{rule}/stats`**) fields **`hit_count`**, **`packet_count`**, **`byte_count`**, **`session_count`**, **`rule_id`**, **`policy_path`**, **`section_id`**, **`domain_name`**, **`transport_node_id`**, **`category`**, **`sequence_number`** via **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** [Splunkbase **4856**]; **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** RULE_TAG verdict flows; **`vmware:nsxt:audit`** rule-change API trails; **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** Manager health; **`vmware:nsxt:gateway_status`** north-south NAT context. Optional **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** [Splunkbase **1810**] for vCenter display-name joins only.
app: **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) parses **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`**, **`syslog`**, **`audit`** and hosts props for NSX 4.2/4.1/3.2; pair **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) for vCenter joins; ship **`vmware:nsxt:rule_stats`** via modular or scripted HTTPS collectors with least-privilege tokens.
spl:
`comment("UC-18.2.6 NSX DFW hit-rate baseline; tune idx; spike>=5x drop<=0.2x")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_sec) earliest=-40d@d latest=now (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:rule_stats" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:firewall")
| eval feed=if(sourcetype=="vmware:nsxt:rule_stats","api_stats","flow_log")
| eval rule_ref=trim(coalesce(rule_id, rule_uuid, rule_path, resource_path, ""))
| eval policy_ref=trim(coalesce(policy_path, security_policy_id, policy_id, ""))
| eval section_ref=trim(coalesce(section_id, dfw_section, firewall_section, ""))
| eval domain_nm=lower(trim(coalesce(domain_name, infra_domain, domain, "default")))
| eval tn_key=trim(coalesce(transport_node_id, tn_uuid, hypervisor_uuid, esxi_host_id, "all-tn"))
| eval cat_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(category, policy_category, "")))
| eval cat_rank=case(match(cat_raw,"ETHERNET"),1, match(cat_raw,"EMERGENCY"),2, match(cat_raw,"INFRASTRUCTURE"),3, match(cat_raw,"ENVIRONMENT"),4, match(cat_raw,"APPLICATION"),5, 1=1,99)
| eval hit_abs=tonumber(coalesce(hit_count, cumulative_hits, hit_total, "0"))
| eval pkt_abs=tonumber(coalesce(packet_count, packets, "0"))
| eval byte_abs=tonumber(coalesce(byte_count, octets, "0"))
| eval sess_abs=tonumber(coalesce(session_count, active_sessions, "0"))
| eval verdict=upper(trim(coalesce(dfw_action, action, firewall_action, "")))
| eval flow_hit=if(feed=="flow_log" AND len(rule_ref)==0 AND len(policy_ref)>0, trim(coalesce(rule_tag, RULE_TAG, "")), rule_ref)
| rex field=flow_hit mode=sed "s/^[^|]*|[^|]*|//"
| eval rule_ref=if(feed=="flow_log" AND len(rule_ref)==0 AND len(flow_hit)>0, flow_hit, rule_ref)
| where len(rule_ref)>0 OR len(policy_ref)>0
| bin _time span=1d aligntime=@d
| stats max(hit_abs) AS hit_eod max(pkt_abs) AS pkt_eod max(byte_abs) AS byte_eod max(sess_abs) AS sess_eod max(cat_rank) AS cat_rank dc(eval(if(feed=="flow_log" AND verdict IN ("ALLOW","ACCEPT","DROP","DENY","REJECT"),verdict,null()))) AS verdict_mix values(cat_raw) AS category_vals BY _time feed rule_ref policy_ref section_ref domain_nm tn_key
| sort 0 feed rule_ref tn_key _time
| streamstats window=1 current=f global=f BY feed rule_ref tn_key last(hit_eod) AS prev_hit
| eval day_delta=if(isnull(prev_hit),null(),hit_eod-prev_hit)
| where day_delta>=0 OR isnull(day_delta)
| eventstats avg(eval(if(_time<relative_time(now(),"-7d@d"),day_delta,null()))) AS b_avg stdev(eval(if(_time<relative_time(now(),"-7d@d"),day_delta,null()))) AS b_sd BY feed rule_ref tn_key
| eval ratio_to_base=if(b_avg>0 AND isnotnull(day_delta), day_delta/b_avg, null())
| eval anomaly_spike=if(isnotnull(ratio_to_base) AND ratio_to_base>=5,1,0)
| eval anomaly_drop=if(isnotnull(ratio_to_base) AND ratio_to_base<=0.2 AND b_avg>10,1,0)
| eval z_raw=if(b_sd>0 AND isnotnull(day_delta),(day_delta-b_avg)/b_sd,null())
| eval effectiveness_hint=case(anomaly_drop==1 AND b_avg>50,"suspected_shadow_or_bypass", anomaly_spike==1,"suspected_scan_or_new_workload", isnotnull(b_avg) AND b_avg>0 AND day_delta==0,"zero_day_activity", 1=1,"within_band")
| eval stale_flag=if(feed=="api_stats" AND effectiveness_hint=="zero_day_activity" AND _time<relative_time(now(),"-30d@d"),1,0)
| where _time>=relative_time(now(),"-14d@d")
| sort - anomaly_spike - anomaly_drop - z_raw
| head 500
| table _time feed domain_nm tn_key policy_ref section_ref rule_ref category_vals cat_rank day_delta b_avg ratio_to_base anomaly_spike anomaly_drop z_raw effectiveness_hint stale_flag pkt_eod byte_eod sess_eod verdict_mix
description: Scores VMware NSX Distributed Firewall effectiveness by baselining per-rule hit_count economics from **`vmware:nsxt:rule_stats`** (Policy statistics APIs) cross-checked with **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`**: daily deltas versus rolling **`b_avg`**, ratio-based **>5×** spikes (possible scan, misconfiguration, or new workload) and **>80%** drops (shadow or bypass hints), **`stale_flag`** tagging long-run **zero_day_activity** on stats feeds, and **`effectiveness_hint`** routing—distinct from UC-18.2.1 distribution volume, UC-18.2.7 revision sync, and UC-18.2.2 NSGroup coverage because this UC asks whether each rule still earns its counter footprint, not whether intent JSON drifted or groups missed endpoints.
value: Hit-counter drift is invisible in ordinary change tickets until auditors or incidents force spreadsheet archaeology across thousands of DFW rows. Turning deltas into prioritized **`rule_ref`** lists compresses segmentation backlog, exposes bypass candidates before PCI/ISO interviews, and gives incident responders a ranked lens on which ALLOW rows suddenly screamed—without mistaking policy publish volume noise (UC-18.2.1) or revision mismatches (UC-18.2.7) for dataplane effectiveness truth.
implementation: Poll Policy statistics into **`vmware:nsxt:rule_stats`**, retain **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** for RULE_TAG triangulation, normalize **`rule_ref`**, schedule the **`spl`** hourly with throttles on **`rule_ref`** + **`domain_nm`**, and refresh **`nsx_dfw_rule_owner.csv`** from ServiceNow or GitOps owners.
visualization: (1) Line chart **`day_delta`** top rules. (2) Scatter **`b_avg`** vs **`day_delta`**. (3) Heatmap **`tn_key`** × **`ratio_to_base`**. (4) Single value **`stale_flag`** count. (5) Table **`z_raw`**, **`pkt_eod`**, **`byte_eod`**, **`verdict_mix`**. (6) Alert preview for **`anomaly_spike`**.
schema: CIM **Network_Traffic** maps **`src_ip`**, **`dst_ip`**, **`action`**, **`transport`**, **`rule`** from **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`**; **Alerts** frames notable-ready rows derived from **`effectiveness_hint`** threshold breaches fed by **`vmware:nsxt:rule_stats`** facts.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Network_Traffic** once FIELDALIAS binds **`vmware:nsxt:firewall`** identities; **Alerts** acceleration optional when ES notables consume **`summary_nsx_dfw_hits`** backfill.

references.title: VMware NSX 4.2 Administration Guide — Security overview

knownFalsePositives:
• **vsip_stats_flush_skew** — Transport-node vsip counter publish can lag NSX Manager REST by minutes after datastore latency bursts, so vmware:nsxt:rule_stats day_delta under-reports a vmware:nsxt:firewall spike. Distinguish with tn_key-scoped timechart alignment; suppress via lookup nsx_dfw_stats_suppress.csv on tn_key and 15m windows when vCenter shows storage latency alarms.

• **rule_save_counter_restart** — Operators clone or touch rules during CAB work; hit_count resets without attack. Join vmware:nsxt:audit request_uri lines on security-policies PATCH keyed to rule_ref; filter with lookup nsx_dfw_change_ticket.csv when change_id matches maintenance.

• **tn_slice_incomplete_rollup** — Pollers sometimes emit one transport_node_id slice per rule_ref, under-counting b_avg until pagination fixes land. Compare dc(tn_key) to expected cluster footprint; page platform on shortfalls, not SecOps.

• **gateway_snat_correlation_bleed** — vmware:nsxt:gateway_status NAT reflow surges during Tier-0 churn can be mistaken for east-west effectiveness loss when dashboards mix scopes. Scope alerts where lookup nsx_logical_scope.csv tags east_west_only=1 on rule_ref.

• **month_end_batch_allow_surge** — Finance batch VMs legitimately 5× daily ALLOW hits on fixed rules. Seasonalize with lookup batch_surge_allowlist.csv rule_ref expect_multiplier; tune macro nsx_dfw_hit_warn_ratio per business unit.

• **policy_rest_shard_lag** — Automation session may read a stale policy shard versus UI during Manager rolling patches. Spot-check two OAuth identities before sev-1; suppress for upgrade_epoch rows in nsx_manager_maint.csv.

• **dual_manager_stat_fork** — Federated twin Manager clusters return different hit_count for shared policy_ref. Require domain_nm in every alert token; split dashboards per manager_cluster_id.

• **deny_only_health_check_ok** — DENY rules with scanner-only hits look hot yet mean healthy blocking. Route hygiene tickets only when stale_flag=1 on ALLOW-intent rows after category_vals review.

• **logging_disabled_phantom_zero** — ALLOW rows without vmware:nsxt:firewall logging look quiet in flows while rule_stats still counts hits. Never retire rules on zero_day_activity alone; demand stats samples or Traceflow proof.
hardware: VMware NSX Distributed Firewall statistics surface on ESXi transport nodes (Dell PowerEdge R650/R750, HPE ProLiant DL360/DL380, Cisco UCS C-Series, Lenovo ThinkSystem) behind NSX Manager clusters running Policy API 4.x; poll HTTPS to Manager VIPs.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) wherever vmware:nsxt:firewall, vmware:nsxt:syslog, and vmware:nsxt:audit parsing runs for VMware NSX 4.2, VMware NSX 4.1, or NSX-T 3.2.x documented under Broadcom NSX lifecycle pages.
• **Rule statistics collectors** — Python modular inputs or scheduled curl jobs authenticate via POST /api/session/create cookie jars or API tokens, then poll GET /policy/api/v1/infra/domains/{domain}/security-policies/{policy}/rules/{rule}/statistics (legacy GET /api/v1/firewall/sections/{section-id}/rules/{rule-id}/stats when MP paths remain). Emit hit_count, packet_count, byte_count, session_count, rule_id, policy_path, section_id, domain_name, transport_node_id, category, sequence_number as structured lines indexed to vmware:nsxt:rule_stats on 15–45 minute cadences with jitter so Manager rate limits stay respected.
• **RBAC** — Service accounts need security-policies read plus domain listing scopes; avoid recycling interactive admin cookies on bastions; vault tokens in Splunk password stanzas with quarterly rotation.
• **Network path** — HTTPS TCP/443 from collectors to NSX Manager VIP without TLS interception that strips SNI; size poll intervals against tens-of-thousands rule counts to avoid HTTP 429 storms during backup windows.
• **Splunk_TA_vmware** optional — ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) only when vmware:vmware:vsphere:vcenter host labels must decode transport_node_id for executives; it is not counted among the five core NSX sourcetypes for this UC contract.
• **Index retention** — Hold ≥90 days on vmware or vmware_nsx indexes so stale_flag math spanning 30 days stays reproducible for auditors.
• **Clock discipline** — Keep NTP skew under ~500 ms across ESXi transport nodes, NSX Manager leaders, and Splunk indexers so day_delta buckets match UI daily columns.

**Step 1 — Wire five sourcetypes for hit economics**
(1) **vmware:nsxt:rule_stats** — Primary cumulative counters from Policy statistics APIs; expect roughly 5–15 KB per rule per poll when JSON is verbose; preserve integers exactly as returned because deltas are the signal.
(2) **vmware:nsxt:firewall** — DFW flow logs with RULE_TAG, src_ip, dst_ip, action for shadow triangulation; turn logging on representative ALLOW rows you plan to score or zero-hit narratives mislead.
(3) **vmware:nsxt:audit** — API mutations on security-policies; map request_uri, user_name, client_ip, outcome to explain counter resets without incidents.
(4) **vmware:nsxt:syslog** — Manager control-plane narratives (upgrades, realization strain) explaining silent poll gaps versus true outages.
(5) **vmware:nsxt:gateway_status** — North-south context so Tier-0 NAT churn does not masquerade as east-west hit-rate drops in combined views.
(6) **props.conf** — KV_MODE=json for stats payloads; TIME_PREFIX on published_at, last_modified_time, or collector ingest_ts; FIELDALIAS toward CIM Network_Traffic on firewall events.
(7) **inputs.conf** — Stagger modular inputs; honor Retry-After on HTTP 429; log throttling in splunkd.log runbooks.

**Step 2 — Promote SPL into saved searches**
Factor the catalogue `spl` block into macro nsx_dfw_rule_hit_base after index naming stabilizes; keep Dashboard Studio and savedsearch uc_18_2_6_nsx_dfw_rule_hit_rate identical.

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.6 NSX DFW hit-rate baseline; tune idx; spike>=5x drop<=0.2x")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_sec) earliest=-40d@d latest=now (sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:rule_stats" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:firewall")
| eval feed=if(sourcetype=="vmware:nsxt:rule_stats","api_stats","flow_log")
| eval rule_ref=trim(coalesce(rule_id, rule_uuid, rule_path, resource_path, ""))
| eval policy_ref=trim(coalesce(policy_path, security_policy_id, policy_id, ""))
| eval section_ref=trim(coalesce(section_id, dfw_section, firewall_section, ""))
| eval domain_nm=lower(trim(coalesce(domain_name, infra_domain, domain, "default")))
| eval tn_key=trim(coalesce(transport_node_id, tn_uuid, hypervisor_uuid, esxi_host_id, "all-tn"))
| eval cat_raw=upper(trim(coalesce(category, policy_category, "")))
| eval cat_rank=case(match(cat_raw,"ETHERNET"),1, match(cat_raw,"EMERGENCY"),2, match(cat_raw,"INFRASTRUCTURE"),3, match(cat_raw,"ENVIRONMENT"),4, match(cat_raw,"APPLICATION"),5, 1=1,99)
| eval hit_abs=tonumber(coalesce(hit_count, cumulative_hits, hit_total, "0"))
| eval pkt_abs=tonumber(coalesce(packet_count, packets, "0"))
| eval byte_abs=tonumber(coalesce(byte_count, octets, "0"))
| eval sess_abs=tonumber(coalesce(session_count, active_sessions, "0"))
| eval verdict=upper(trim(coalesce(dfw_action, action, firewall_action, "")))
| eval flow_hit=if(feed=="flow_log" AND len(rule_ref)==0 AND len(policy_ref)>0, trim(coalesce(rule_tag, RULE_TAG, "")), rule_ref)
| rex field=flow_hit mode=sed "s/^[^|]*|[^|]*|//"
| eval rule_ref=if(feed=="flow_log" AND len(rule_ref)==0 AND len(flow_hit)>0, flow_hit, rule_ref)
| where len(rule_ref)>0 OR len(policy_ref)>0
| bin _time span=1d aligntime=@d
| stats max(hit_abs) AS hit_eod max(pkt_abs) AS pkt_eod max(byte_abs) AS byte_eod max(sess_abs) AS sess_eod max(cat_rank) AS cat_rank dc(eval(if(feed=="flow_log" AND verdict IN ("ALLOW","ACCEPT","DROP","DENY","REJECT"),verdict,null()))) AS verdict_mix values(cat_raw) AS category_vals BY _time feed rule_ref policy_ref section_ref domain_nm tn_key
| sort 0 feed rule_ref tn_key _time
| streamstats window=1 current=f global=f BY feed rule_ref tn_key last(hit_eod) AS prev_hit
| eval day_delta=if(isnull(prev_hit),null(),hit_eod-prev_hit)
| where day_delta>=0 OR isnull(day_delta)
| eventstats avg(eval(if(_time<relative_time(now(),"-7d@d"),day_delta,null()))) AS b_avg stdev(eval(if(_time<relative_time(now(),"-7d@d"),day_delta,null()))) AS b_sd BY feed rule_ref tn_key
| eval ratio_to_base=if(b_avg>0 AND isnotnull(day_delta), day_delta/b_avg, null())
| eval anomaly_spike=if(isnotnull(ratio_to_base) AND ratio_to_base>=5,1,0)
| eval anomaly_drop=if(isnotnull(ratio_to_base) AND ratio_to_base<=0.2 AND b_avg>10,1,0)
| eval z_raw=if(b_sd>0 AND isnotnull(day_delta),(day_delta-b_avg)/b_sd,null())
| eval effectiveness_hint=case(anomaly_drop==1 AND b_avg>50,"suspected_shadow_or_bypass", anomaly_spike==1,"suspected_scan_or_new_workload", isnotnull(b_avg) AND b_avg>0 AND day_delta==0,"zero_day_activity", 1=1,"within_band")
| eval stale_flag=if(feed=="api_stats" AND effectiveness_hint=="zero_day_activity" AND _time<relative_time(now(),"-30d@d"),1,0)
| where _time>=relative_time(now(),"-14d@d")
| sort - anomaly_spike - anomaly_drop - z_raw
| head 500
| table _time feed domain_nm tn_key policy_ref section_ref rule_ref category_vals cat_rank day_delta b_avg ratio_to_base anomaly_spike anomaly_drop z_raw effectiveness_hint stale_flag pkt_eod byte_eod sess_eod verdict_mix
```

**Understanding this SPL**
• **feed lanes** — api_stats carries official cumulative hit_count for delta math; flow_log fills RULE_TAG gaps when policy paths rename faster than pollers refresh.
• **Daily bin** — End-of-day max(hit_abs) approximates NSX UI daily Hit Count columns; switch span=1h for intra-day hunts if analysts demand finer buckets.
• **eventstats baseline** — b_avg and b_sd ignore the latest seven days so a spike does not normalize itself; widen the exclusion window to 14 days for very stable tenants.
• **Thresholds** — ratio_to_base>=5 flags horizontal scans, miswired ALLOW storms, or new workloads; ratio_to_base<=0.2 with b_avg>10 marks >80% drops hinting shadow rules or bypass paths; raise b_avg floor if micro-segmentation is naturally bursty.
• **effectiveness_hint** — Maps to suspected_shadow_or_bypass, suspected_scan_or_new_workload, zero_day_activity, within_band runbook queues; distinct from UC-18.2.7 revision drift and UC-18.2.2 NSGroup coverage.
• **cat_rank** — Encodes Ethernet > Emergency > Infrastructure > Environment > Application precedence so investigations respect NSX category ordering when debating shadowing.
• **stale_flag** — api_stats rows with zero_day_activity older than 30 days become retirement candidates only after human review with UC-18.2.1 volume context.
• **verdict_mix** — Distinct verdict tallies from flow logs highlight deny-only health versus mixed verdict anomalies.

**Step 3 — Validate in VMware consoles**
(1) **UI parity** — NSX Manager Security → Distributed Firewall → pick domain_nm → policy → Rules tab; compare Hit Count and packet columns for rule_ref against Splunk day_delta and pkt_eod for the same UTC day boundary.
(2) **API replay** — curl the statistics URI with the same bearer token as the collector; diff hit_count at aligned timestamps to prove no double ingestion hosts.
(3) **Gap detection** — timechart span=15m count over vmware:nsxt:rule_stats exposes silent collectors; flatline longer than twice poll interval without change ticket flags data paths first.
(4) **Category ladder** — category_vals must match UI Ethernet/Application labels; fix FIELDALIAS if cat_rank scores look reversed.
(5) **Counter reset lab** — Edit a test rule; verify negative day_delta rows vanish after the where day_delta>=0 guard and audit correlation fires as expected.
(6) **Traceflow** — When effectiveness_hint=suspected_shadow_or_bypass, run vendor Traceflow to prove which higher sequence steals flows.

**Step 4 — Operationalise**
(1) **Dashboard row A** — Single-value stale_flag counts and anomaly_spike density per domain_nm for leadership KPI tiles.
(2) **Row B** — Line chart day_delta for top 20 rule_ref movers with referenceLine at configurable multiples of b_avg via macro nsx_dfw_spike_mult.
(3) **Row C** — Scatter b_avg vs day_delta colored by effectiveness_hint for SOC analysts.
(4) **Row D** — Heatmap tn_key × ratio_to_base limited to feed=api_stats to show vsip skew clusters.
(5) **Summary index** — Hourly collect into summary_nsx_dfw_hits for 30-day retros without rescanning 40d raw every drilldown.
(6) **RBAC** — splunk_role_nsx_seg_engineer edits macros; splunk_role_soc reads plus notable creation when Enterprise Security is licensed.
(7) **Runbooks** — Link internal microsegmentation hygiene guides, PCI evidence assembly, and Broadcom SR templates when Manager UI disagrees with REST counters.

**Step 5 — Visualization, alert design, and troubleshooting**
**Visualization** — Dashboard Studio worksheet ds_nsx_dfw_rule_effectiveness with brush-linked tables; prefer ranked bars and timelines over pies when cardinality is high.

**Alert design** — Critical when anomaly_spike=1 AND b_avg>100 AND effectiveness_hint=suspected_scan_or_new_workload without nsx_dfw_change_ticket.csv match. High when anomaly_drop=1 on lookup-tagged ALLOW rows. Warning when stale_flag=1 for Application category cleanup queues. Throttle on composite key rule_ref plus domain_nm for 12 hours. Use nsx_logical_scope.csv to drop gateway_snat_correlation_bleed noise.

**Troubleshooting**
• **Silent vmware:nsxt:rule_stats** — Inspect collector HTTP 401/403, wrong domain scope, proxy MITM, or license tier disabling statistics; read python.log traces.
• **All ratio_to_base null** — b_avg zero from short earliest or brand-new rules; extend to 40d or seed nsx_dfw_hit_seed_baseline.csv.
• **Cluster-wide spike post-upgrade** — Counters reset during transport upgrades; correlate vmware:nsxt:syslog upgrade banners with Splunk bursts before declaring incidents.
• **Duplicate rule_ref** — Collisions across domains; always print domain_nm + policy_ref in alert text.
• **Noisy verdict_mix** — Logging profiles or duplicate flows; coordinate with UC-18.2.1 owners before muting.
• **Search auto-finalize** — Reduce head window or query summary index first on busy multisite clusters.

**Compact SPL notes** — **Index union** wraps vmware, vmware_nsx, network_sec; **coalesce** absorbs renames; **bin** @d aligns audit days; **streamstats** isolates prev_hit per key; **baseline** uses seven-day holdout; **ratio** stays unitless; **z_raw** aids sorts; **throttle** keys on rule_ref plus domain_nm; **summary** cuts DMA; **pagination** guards coverage.

**Glossary** — **vsip**: dataplane module owning DFW counters; **hit_count**: cumulative matches; **packet_count** / **byte_count**: byte economics; **RULE_TAG**: logged rule tuple; **day_delta**: estimated daily increment; **b_avg** / **b_sd**: rolling mean and stddev of daily increments; **shadow**: higher-sequence rule wins flow; **bypass**: traffic skips intended path; **Ethernet** / **Emergency** / **Infrastructure** / **Environment** / **Application**: NSX category ladder; **Policy API**: intent REST; **MP API**: legacy manager REST; **transport_node_id**: ESXi TN UUID; **NSX Manager**: control cluster VIP; **Traceflow**: path proof; **OAuth**: token auth; **summary index**: rolled-up metrics; **splunkd.log**: daemon log; **python.log**: modular input log; **CIM Network_Traffic**: normalized flows; **CIM Alerts**: notable frame.
grandmaExplanation: We read the trip meter on each numbered east-west rule—when it races far ahead or suddenly idles next to its usual month, you get a plain nudge before clutter or real trouble piles up.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Production ALLOW row rule_ref=5012 shows day_delta jumping from thirty-day b_avg≈900 to day_delta=5200 in one UTC day while vmware:nsxt:audit stays quiet; ratio_to_base exceeds five, anomaly_spike=1, z_raw>4, matching port-scan chatter in vmware:nsxt:firewall.
controlTest.negativeScenario: During CAB NSX upgrade, vmware:nsxt:audit logs a burst of RULE_MODIFIED events and counters reset cluster-wide, but change_ticket CHG44521 carries DFW_STATS_BASELINE_RESET; operators suppress twelve hours via nsx_dfw_stats_suppress.csv and no spike page fires post-window.
